task:
You are porting one TypeScript module to Python for the pycortex project (a mechanical 1:1 port of hoocode). Do NOT add features. Feature parity only. Match file structure to the TS original for diffability.

## Task
Port the TS source file:
  /Users/sachinkoli/github/hoocode/packages/ai/src/providers/faux.ts
to the Python target file (CREATE it):
  /Users/sachinkoli/github/pycortex/packages/ai/provider-faux/src/cortex/ai/providers/faux.py

Read the TS source in full first. Also read its test to understand the contract:
  /Users/sachinkoli/github/hoocode/packages/ai/test/faux-provider.test.ts

Do NOT write any test file — another agent handles tests. Only create faux.py.

## Available ported dependencies (import from these; do NOT redefine)
From `cortex.ai.types` (pydantic v2 models, snake_case fields):
- TextContent(type="text", text: str, text_signature: str|None=None)
- ThinkingContent(type="thinking", thinking: str, thinking_signature=None, redacted=None)
- ImageContent(type="image", data: str, mime_type: str)
- ToolCall(type="toolCall", id: str, name: str, arguments: dict[str,Any], thought_signature=None)
- Usage(input:int, output:int, cache_read:int, cache_write:int, total_tokens:int, cost: dict[str,float])
- UserMessage(role="user", content: str|list[TextContent|ImageContent], timestamp:int)
- AssistantMessage(role="assistant", content: list[TextContent|ThinkingContent|ToolCall], api:str, provider:str, model:str, usage:Usage, stop_reason:StopReason, error_message:str|None=None, response_id:str|None=None, timestamp:int, ...others optional)
- ToolResultMessage(role="toolResult", tool_call_id:str, tool_name:str, content:list[TextContent|ImageContent], is_error:bool, timestamp:int)
- Message = UserMessage | AssistantMessage | ToolResultMessage
- Tool(name:str, description:str, parameters:dict[str,Any])
- Context(system_prompt:str|None=None, messages:list[Message], tools:list[Tool]|None=None)
- Model(id, name, api, provider, base_url, reasoning:bool, input:list[Literal["text","image"]], cost:dict[str,float], context_window:int, max_tokens:int, ...)
- StreamOptions(temperature=None, max_tokens=None, signal:Any|None=None, api_key=None, transport=None, cache_retention:CacheRetention|None=None, session_id:str|None=None). NOTE StreamOptions has NO on_response field in the Python port — the TS `options.onResponse` call: guard it as `getattr(options, "onResponse", None)` won't exist; SKIP the onResponse call entirely since the Python StreamOptions/SimpleStreamOptions has no such field. (Feature parity note: it's a no-op in tests.)
- SimpleStreamOptions(model, api, provider, api_key=None, base_url=None)
- StopReason = Literal["stop","length","toolUse","error","aborted"]
- CacheRetention type
- Event models (all have a `.type` Literal and snake_case fields):
  StartEvent(partial), TextStartEvent(content_index, partial), TextDeltaEvent(content_index, delta, partial), TextEndEvent(content_index, content, partial), ThinkingStartEvent(content_index, partial), ThinkingDeltaEvent(content_index, delta, partial), ThinkingEndEvent(content_index, content, partial), ToolCallStartEvent(content_index, partial), ToolCallDeltaEvent(content_index, delta, partial), ToolCallEndEvent(content_index, tool_call, partial), DoneEvent(reason:Literal["stop","length","toolUse"], message), ErrorEvent(reason:Literal["aborted","error"], error)

From `cortex.ai.stream`:
- AssistantMessageEventStream (has .push(event), .end(result))
- create_assistant_message_event_stream() -> AssistantMessageEventStream

From `cortex.ai.models`:
- ApiProvider (constructor: ApiProvider(api:str, stream, stream_simple))
- register_api_provider(provider: ApiProvider, source_id: str|None=None)
- unregister_api_providers(source_id: str)
The stream functions registered have signature: (model: Model, context: Context, options: dict|None) -> AssistantMessageEventStream. IMPORTANT: in the Python port, `options` passed into a registered stream fn is `StreamOptions | None` (an object, not a dict) — the api_registry wrapper just forwards it. Treat options as a StreamOptions|None object with attributes.

## Key translation rules
- snake_case all public names: fauxText->faux_text, fauxThinking->faux_thinking, fauxToolCall->faux_tool_call, fauxAssistantMessage->faux_assistant_message, registerFauxProvider->register_faux_provider. Return-value dict keys become the pydantic models above (return AssistantMessage(...), TextContent(...), etc.).
- The registration return object (FauxProviderRegistration) should be a Python class/dataclass exposing: api (str), models (list[Model]), get_model(model_id: str|None=None) -> Model|None, state (a mutable object with .call_count int — use a small class or a dict; tests access registration.state.call_count so use an object with attribute call_count), set_responses(responses), append_responses(responses), get_pending_response_count() -> int, unregister(). Also expose the response step factory type.
- FauxResponseStep = AssistantMessage | Callable. A factory is called as step(context, options, state, model) and may be sync or async (await if coroutine — use `inspect.iscoroutine` on result or `asyncio.iscoroutinefunction`). Return AssistantMessage.
- `structuredClone(message)` -> `message.model_copy(deep=True)`.
- `Date.now()` -> `int(time.time() * 1000)`.
- `Math.random()` -> `random.random()`; `Math.floor`->int(...), `Math.ceil`->math.ceil, `Math.max/min`->max/min.
- `JSON.stringify(x)` -> `json.dumps(x, separators=(",", ":"))` to match JS compact output (no spaces). For serializeContext token estimates the exact bytes matter — JS JSON.stringify uses no spaces by default, so use separators=(",", ":") and ensure_ascii=False. For tool arguments streaming (splitStringByTokenSize(JSON.stringify(block.arguments))) same compact form. IMPORTANT: for the `tools` serialization `JSON.stringify([tool])`, the tool includes `parameters` (a JSON schema dict) — dump the tool as its plain dict. Use tool.model_dump() but note JS serializes camelCase-less plain objects; since the test computes expectedPromptTokens from the TS side it won't run against Python — the Python test will compute its own expected value. So just be internally consistent: serialize each Tool via json.dumps of a plain dict representation.
- `queueMicrotask(async () => {...})` -> schedule the async body as a task: `asyncio.ensure_future(_run())` inside the stream function (which is sync and returns the outer stream immediately). Use `asyncio.get_event_loop()`/`ensure_future`. The coroutine does the work and pushes to the stream.
- `scheduleChunk`: if tokensPerSecond falsy/<=0 -> `await asyncio.sleep(0)`; else `await asyncio.sleep(delay_ms/1000)` where delay_ms = estimate_tokens(chunk)/tokensPerSecond*1000.
- AbortSignal: Python StreamOptions.signal is `Any|None`. Represent abort as an object with a boolean `.aborted` attribute (tests will pass a simple object/None). Check `signal is not None and getattr(signal, "aborted", False)`. Do not import any abort library.
- Preserve the exact event ordering and stopReason logic (error/aborted terminal handling) from streamWithDeltas.
- Preserve prompt-cache logic (per session_id, cache_retention != "none", common prefix length).
- randomId(prefix) -> f"{prefix}:{int(time.time()*1000)}:{random-base36}" ; for base36 use e.g. a helper converting random to base36 string like the TS `Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)` — approximate with a random alphanumeric token; exact value is not asserted.
- DEFAULT_USAGE: define as a module-level constant; when cloning to messages, produce a fresh copy (deep) so shared mutation doesn't happen — in TS it's a shared object literal, but to be safe return copies.

## Module __init__ export
Also update the package __init__ so the public API is importable. Create/replace:
  /Users/sachinkoli/github/pycortex/packages/ai/provider-faux/src/cortex/ai/providers/__init__.py
It should be minimal: just a namespace init (can be empty, or re-export from faux). Since this is a PEP420-style namespace under cortex.ai, check how sibling packages do it: `cortex.ai.stream/__init__.py` re-exports its public names. Create `providers/__init__.py` that re-exports the faux public names: faux_text, faux_thinking, faux_tool_call, faux_assistant_message, register_faux_provider, and the type aliases FauxModelDefinition, FauxContentBlock, FauxResponseFactory, FauxResponseStep, RegisterFauxProviderOptions, FauxProviderRegistration. Follow the exact __all__ style used in cortex/ai/stream/__init__.py.

Do NOT create any cortex/__init__.py or cortex/ai/__init__.py (namespace packages, none exist for siblings — verify by listing packages/ai/stream/src/cortex).

## Deliverable
- Create faux.py (the full port) and providers/__init__.py.
- Do not run tests (another agent owns tests; integration is run by the orchestrator).
- Run `cd /Users/sachinkoli/github/pycortex && uv run ruff check packages/ai/provider-faux && uv run ruff format packages/ai/provider-faux` to ensure your file is lint-clean and formatted. Fix any issues.
- Report: the final path(s) you wrote, and any deviations from the TS you had to make (especially around onResponse omission, options object shape, abort signal representation).
